growth_records.cite: Becker et al. Co-culture and biogeography of Prochlorococcus and SAR11. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0365-4. [PMC6775983]
growth_records.snippet: The growth rate of SAR11 increased from 0.35 d−1 (s = 0.01 d−1) when grown in AMS1 medium to 0.41 d−1 (s = 0.01 d−1) after acclimation to ProMS
growth_records.notes: Reported specific growth rate 0.35 d-1 in AMS1. Convert to per-hour: 0.35/24 = 0.01458 h-1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu = 24*ln2/0.35 = 0.693/0.01458 = 47.5 h. AMS1 base recipe cited to reference (not numerically given); only organic amendments (pyruvate 50 uM, glycine 50 uM, methionine 10 uM) are stated in text for SAR11 transfer medium. No uptake/secretion fluxes reported (ecology paper). Temp 22 C, 12 umol photons m-2 s-1 constant light from maintenance methods.

growth_records.cite: Becker et al. Co-culture and biogeography of Prochlorococcus and SAR11. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0365-4. [PMC6775983]
growth_records.snippet: The growth rate of SAR11 increased from 0.35 d−1 ... to 0.41 d−1 (s = 0.01 d−1) after acclimation to ProMS; however, the maximum cell abundance decreased from 1.1 × 10^8 cells ml−1 to 7.5 × 10^6 cells ml−1
growth_records.notes: Reported specific growth rate 0.41 d-1 in ProMS monoculture. Per-hour: 0.41/24 = 0.01708 h-1. td = 24*ln2/0.41 = 0.693/0.01708 = 40.6 h. Max cell abundance 7.5e6 cells/ml (ProMS) vs 1.1e8 cells/ml (AMS1). In co-culture with Prochlorococcus SAR11 grew 15-70% faster (abstract/Fig4), i.e. ~0.47-0.70 d-1 (=0.41*1.15 to 0.41*1.70); exact per-strain co-culture rates only in Fig4, not in text. Medium base = Sargasso seawater (composition not numerically stated) + Pro99 nutrients + stated organics pyruvate 1 uM, glycine 1 uM, methionine 0.2 uM + 9 vitamins. No fluxes reported.
